title: carthamus glaucus var. alexandrinus
intro: Carthamus glaucus var. alexandrinus, commonly known as Roman chamomile, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and soothing restlessness, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas and skincare products. Roman chamomile can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from insomnia and digestive problems to skin conditions and menstrual cramps.
